zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel le Despenser於大約1285年出生在英國時，她的父親Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester25歲，母親Lady Isabella de Beauchamp38歲。她於1275年7月15日與John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1334年12月4日在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年50歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel le Despenser于大约1285年出生在英国时，她的父亲Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester25岁，母亲Lady Isabella de Beauchamp38岁。她于1275年7月15日与John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings在英国英格兰艾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1334年12月4日在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里享年50岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel le Despenser was born about 1285, in England, her father, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, was 25 and her mother, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, was 38. She married John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings on 15 July 1275, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 4 December 1334, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England.

fr.Salisbury St Edmund, Wiltshire, England,: Salisbury St Edmund, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel le Despenser est née environ 1285, à Angleterre, son père, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings le 15 juillet 1275, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 4 décembre 1334, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel le Despenser um 1285 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester 25 und ihre Mutter Lady Isabella de Beauchamp 38. Sie hat am 15. Juli 1275 in Essex, England, United Kingdom John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 4. Dezember 1334 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel le Despenser è nata il circa 1285 nel England, sua padre, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings il 15 luglio 1275, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 4 dicembre 1334, nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Salisbury, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel le Despenserが約1285年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchesterは25歳，母Lady Isabella de Beauchampは38歳であった。1275年7月15日にイギリスイギリスエセックスでJohn Hastings 1st Baron Hastingsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1334年12月4日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーで50歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Salisbury St Edmund, Wiltshire, England,: 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이 스트 에드먼드
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel le Despenser(이)가 대략 1285년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Isabella de Beauchamp(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1275년 7월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1334년 12월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel le Despenser nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings em 15 de julho de 1275, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 4 de dezembro de 1334, em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Salisbury St Edmund, Wiltshire, England,: салисбури ст эдмунд, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel le Despenser родилась около 1285 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings 15 июля 1275 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 4 декабря 1334 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel le Despenser nació aproximadamente 1285, en Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tenía 38 años. Se casó con John Hastings 1st Baron Hastings el 15 de julio de 1275, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 4 de diciembre de 1334, en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
